edges.description: The same motor-neuronal degeneration extends to lower cranial nerve motor nuclei, producing bulbar dysfunction.
edges.source: Lower Motor Neuron and Anterior Horn Degeneration

edges.description: Auditory pathway involvement parallels the lower cranial nerve and brainstem pathology, manifesting as sensorineural hearing loss.

nodes.description: A hallmark and near-universal feature of MMND is sensorineural hearing loss; all patients in the largest series had clinical and/or audiological evidence of hearing impairment, and the deficit has been characterized as an auditory neuropathy. Impaired hearing is frequently among the predominant initial manifestations.

evidence.reference_title: Madras motor neuron disease (MMND): clinical description and survival pattern of 116 patients from Southern India seen over 36 years (1971-2007).
evidence.snippet: All patients had clinical and/or audiological evidence of hearing impairment.

evidence.reference_title: Madras motor neuron disease (MMND): clinical description and survival pattern of 116 patients from Southern India seen over 36 years (1971-2007).
evidence.snippet: young age of onset and presence of auditory neuropathy

nodes.description: The core lesion of MMND is degeneration of lower motor neurons in the spinal anterior horn, producing wasting and weakness of the limbs (predominantly distal) on a background of young onset. Electromyography and nerve conduction studies in MMND patients support lower-motor-neuron involvement, and the clinical picture has features of amyotrophic lateral sclerosis but with a much younger age of onset.
nodes.id: Lower Motor Neuron and Anterior Horn Degeneration

evidence.reference_title: Madras motor neuron disease (MMND): clinical description and survival pattern of 116 patients from Southern India seen over 36 years (1971-2007).
evidence.snippet: The characteristic features are onset in young, weakness and wasting of limbs, multiple lower cranial nerve palsies and sensorineural hearing

evidence.reference_title: Unveiling a Rare Case: Madras Motor Neuron Disease in an 18-Year-Old Patient.
evidence.snippet: Neurological examination revealed signs consistent with lower motor neuron involvement.

nodes.description: MMND characteristically involves multiple lower cranial nerves, particularly the seventh (facial) and the ninth to twelfth, producing facial weakness, tongue wasting and fasciculation, dysarthria and dysphagia. Bulbar palsy is an invariable feature of the MMND variant and is present in a substantial fraction of classic MMND.

evidence.reference_title: Madras motor neuron disease variant, clinical features of seven patients.
evidence.snippet: multiple cranial nerve palsies particularly the seventh, ninth to twelfth and sensorineural hearing loss

evidence.reference_title: Madras motor neuron disease variant, clinical features of seven patients.
evidence.snippet: Bulbar palsy was an invariable feature, being present in all patients compared to 38.3% of MMND
